items.title: BUG: stats.levy_stable: fix PDF in Tails for `beta=+-1`

labels.name: defect
labels.description: A clear bug or issue that prevents SciPy from being installed or used as expected

items.body: #### Reference issue Closes #20636 by fixing integration. #### What does this implement/fix? The PDF of the Levy Stable distribution did not produce the correct results in the far tails for $\beta = \pm 1$. This PR fixes that as show in the graph where the y-axis is the log of the pdf Furthermore, for the S1 parameterization with $\alpha\approx 1$ and $\beta=\pm 1$, the PDF was wildly off as shown in the graph where the y-axis is the log of the pdf With this PR, the above graph looks like #### Additional information Correcting these issues was accomplished with two main fixes. First, I modified the points used to integrate in the function `_pdf_single_value_piecewise_post_rounding_Z0`. The integrand in this function goes from very peaked for small $x$ to somewhat broad for large $x$. The previous method to get the integration points worked for an integrand that was peaked but not broad. To fix this, I caught when the previous technique broke down and then chose evenly spaced integration points which works well for a broad integrand. For very small $x$, the integrand becomes so peaked that integration is hopeless. To fix this, I used a Taylor Series for small $x$ which fixes the "step" behavior we see in the second graph above. One issue is that this implementation is about 2-3x slower than the previous implementation. This could probably be fixed by vectorizing the function `g` in [`levyst.c`](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/main/scipy/stats/_levy_stable/c_src/levyst.c), but I figured I'd wait for a review before trying that.

items.title: Refactor legacy PyDefense game to modern Python 3 and Pygame
items.body:
I have refactored the 12-year-old PyDefense game to work with modern Python 3 and the latest Pygame. 

Key changes include:
- **Project Structure**: Moved source code into a `pydefense` package and created a clean `run_game.py` entry point. Added `requirements.txt`.
- **Modernization**: Updated Python 2 syntax (`print`, `xrange`, exception handling, `unichr`, etc.) to Python 3.
- **Dependencies**: Removed hardcoded path appending for `pytmx` and configured it as a standard dependency. Updated `pytmx` usage to be compatible with newer versions (removed `pixelalpha` argument).
- **Bug Fixes**: Fixed issues with mutable class attributes (images list in Towers) and ensured safe asset path resolution.
- **Verification**: Verified the game runs successfully in a headless environment, capturing screenshots of the title and level scenes.

The game is now playable and maintainable in a modern development environment.

items.title: v137.16.2 — Deterministic Codebase Topology Indexer
items.body:
### Motivation
- Provide an additive, deterministic topology indexing layer that extends the deterministic memory graph and decision DAG compiler to produce a stable codebase topology artifact. 
- Ensure topology indexing obeys the repository's determinism laws: same input → same bytes/hash/traversal with no hidden randomness or wall-clock dependence. 
- Support canonical, replay-safe indexing of modules, packages, release arcs, DAG lineage, proof dependencies and test coverage edges while preserving decoder and lower-layer invariants. 

### Description
- Add `src/qec/memory/codebase_topology_indexer.py` which implements frozen deterministic dataclasses `TopologyIndexNode`, `TopologyIndexEdge`, `CodebaseTopologyIndex`, `TopologyIndexValidationReport`, and `TopologyIndexExecutionReceipt` with `to_dict()`, `to_canonical_json()`, `to_canonical_bytes()`, and `as_hash_payload()` methods. 
- Implement deterministic normalization (`normalize_codebase_topology_input()`), builder (`build_codebase_topology_index()`), validator (`validate_codebase_topology_index()`), and traversal (`traverse_codebase_topology_index()`) functions that enforce stable field ordering, fail-fast validation, and canonical SHA-256 hashing. 
- Enforce supported node kinds and relationship kinds, lineage hash format, non-negative weights, uniqueness of IDs, and hierarchy constraints, and produce stable ordered nodes/edges using explicit sort keys. 
- Add focused tests in `tests/test_codebase_topology_indexer.py` that exercise repeated-run byte/hash identity, duplicate-node/edge rejection, invalid hierarchy rejection, deterministic hierarchy/dependency/lineage traversals, canonical export stability, and validation report semantics. 
- Files changed: `src/qec/memory/codebase_topology_indexer.py`, `tests/test_codebase_topology_indexer.py`. 

### Testing
- Ran the new test module with `pytest -q tests/test_codebase_topology_indexer.py` which produced: `9 passed in 0.51s`. 
- The test suite includes repeated-run determinism checks that assert byte-identical canonical bytes and identical index/traversal hashes and all such checks passed. 
- Validation tests asserting duplicate node/edge rejection and invalid hierarchy rejection were executed and behaved as expected. 
- All added tests passed and the change is additive and isolated to the new topology indexer module.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69dd744c0b988332846ac9a033dfd63e)

## Summary by Sourcery

Introduce a deterministic codebase topology indexing layer that builds, validates, and traverses a stable graph of codebase structure and lineage.

New Features:
- Add immutable topology index data structures for nodes, edges, index metadata, validation reports, and traversal receipts with canonical serialization and hashing.
- Provide normalization and index-building utilities that enforce deterministic ordering, schema constraints, and canonical SHA-256 index hashes.
- Add traversal support over the topology index for hierarchy, dependency, lineage, and coverage modes with deterministic visitation order and traversal hashes.

Tests:
- Add focused tests covering deterministic index construction and traversal, duplicate and invalid reference rejection, hierarchy validation, canonical export stability, and validation report behavior.

items.title: Support for MultiGriddedData
items.body:
## Change Summary
Adds possibility to read multiple model files, e.g. uEMEP gridded city simulation. This is done through the new MultiGriddedData, which holds a list of GriddedData files. As of now, only functions that are needed for colocation using colocate_gridded_ungridded are supported.

While trying to have as small a footprint as possible, some changes to the ungridded container was needed, to make sure the regridding works correctly for both gridded and ungridded data.

Assumes model file have the same projection (or lack of projection), vars, units, ts_type and ndim. The data must also be disjoint (no overlapping).

Is tested with uEMEP and EMEP data

- Full run started:
  - Output will (eventually) be available here: https://aeroval-test.met.no/thlun/pages/evaluation/?project=uemep&experiment=uemep-2025-08-29

✅ All Python files compile successfully with Python 3.12.3

items.title: list -> iterable builtins
items.body:
In Python 3:
* `itertools.imap`, `itertools.ifilter`, `itertools.izip`, `dict.iterkeys`, `dict.itervalues` and `dict.iteritems` have been removed entirely
* the formerly eager (list-returning) map, filter, zip, dict.keys, dict.values and dict.items now returns lazy iterators (or view objects)

While this is not problematic when just iterating, there are numerous cases where the results are expected/used as lists which would break in P3.

=> convert all code using these functions to semantics-matching cross-version helpers (so that the behaviour is the same between P2 and P3), and ban the builtins via pylint to avoid them cropping back up.

Removed the `range` shim following discussion on `xrange` conversion commit as P3's `range` is a fairly complete Sequence object (though not a list) and `range` seems to more or less never be used for anything other than iteration, so the risk of incompatibility looks very remote.

issue #8530

items.title: fix: improve fcc tests branch filtering pattern
items.body:
The FCC file has these branches/subbranches:
```
 'PARAMETERS',
 'PARAMETERS/_intMap',
 'PARAMETERS/_intMap/_intMap.first',
 'PARAMETERS/_intMap/_intMap.second',
 'PARAMETERS/_floatMap',
 'PARAMETERS/_floatMap/_floatMap.first',
 'PARAMETERS/_floatMap/_floatMap.second',
 'PARAMETERS/_stringMap',
 'PARAMETERS/_stringMap/_stringMap.first',
 'PARAMETERS/_stringMap/_stringMap.second'
 ```
When we do `tree.iteritems()` to extract the base form, subbranch names only contain the subbranch part and not the full path. The function sees PARAMETERS first. Then it sees _intMap, then it sees _intMap.first and so on. Therefore, the filtering function `lambda x: "PARAMETERS" not in x` is not enough. I'm updating it to a regex to exclude `_*Map*` patterns too.

items.body:
## Overview

This PR significantly enhances EPCP3-O's core skills with production-ready, advanced capabilities across four major areas:

1. **Advanced Test Generation** - Integration, regression, and edge case tests
2. **Enhanced Refactoring** - Language idioms and automated code migrations
3. **Automated Code Review** - Quality scoring and PR feedback generation (NEW)
4. **Distributed Self-Healing** - Cluster monitoring and service mesh support

## 🎯 What's New

### 1. Advanced Test Generation (`tester.py`)

The test generator now goes far beyond basic unit tests:

```python
# Generate integration tests with mocking
integration_tests = await tester.generate_integration_tests(code)

# Generate regression tests to prevent bugs
regression_tests = await tester.generate_regression_tests(code, known_issues)

# Support multiple frameworks
pytest_tests = await tester.generate_tests(code, framework="pytest")

# Edge case coverage
edge_tests = await tester.generate_tests(code, test_type="edge_case")
```

**Features:**
- Smart code analysis extracts functions, classes, and dependencies
- Automatic detection of async code, databases, APIs, and file I/O
- Generates appropriate mocks for external dependencies
- Support for both unittest and pytest frameworks
- Comprehensive edge case testing (null, empty, boundary, unicode, special characters)

### 2. Enhanced Refactoring (`refactor.py`)

Language-specific idioms and automated migrations:

```python
# Apply Python idioms (enumerate, is/is not, context managers)
refactored = await refactorer.apply_language_idioms(code, "python")

# Migrate Python 2 to Python 3 automatically
migration = await refactorer.migrate_code(code, "python2to3")
# Converts print statements, xrange, dict.iteritems(), etc.

# Fix code smells
cleaned = await refactorer.fix_code_smells(code, "python")
```

**Supported Languages:**
- **Python:** enumerate, is/is not comparisons, list comprehensions, context managers
- **JavaScript:** let/const, arrow functions, template literals
- **Java:** diamond operator, try-with-resources
- **Go:** defer, goroutines

**Migration Paths:**
- Python 2 → 3 (print, xrange, dict methods, unicode, raw_input)
- ES5 → ES6 (var to const/let, arrow functions, classes)
- Java 8 → 11 (var, new String/collection methods)

### 3. Automated Code Review (`reviewer.py`) - NEW

Complete automated code review system with quality scoring:

```python
# Comprehensive code review
review = await reviewer.review_code(code, language="python")
print(f"Quality Score: {review['overall_score']}/100")
print(f"Approval: {review['approval_status']}")

- Security checks: eval/exec usage, bare except, mutable defaults
- Complexity analysis: function length, nesting depth
- Best practices: print statements, magic numbers, TODO comments
- Documentation checking: missing docstrings
- PR feedback with approval recommendations

### 4. Distributed Self-Healing (`healer.py`)

Enterprise-grade self-healing for distributed systems:

```python
# Monitor cluster health
health = await healer.monitor_cluster_health(cluster_config)

# Register services dynamically
await healer.register_service("api-gateway", {
    "address": "10.0.0.1",
    "port": 8000,
    "health_endpoint": "/health"
})

# Auto-heal distributed systems
results = await healer.heal_distributed_system(health)

# Service mesh integration

```

**Features:**
- Deployment type awareness (standalone, clustered, distributed, microservices)
- Cluster-wide health monitoring
- Service discovery and registration
- Circuit breaker pattern for resilience
- Distributed transaction recovery with rollback
- Network partition detection and handling
- Service mesh integration (Istio, Linkerd)

items.title: Fix Travis CI Build Issues and Improve Docker/Testing Infrastructure
items.body:
# Fix Travis CI Build Issues and Improve Docker/Testing Infrastructure

## Summary
This PR fixes multiple issues preventing the Travis CI build from completing successfully, including test failures, Docker build hangs, and code quality issues. It also updates dependencies and improves the overall build and testing infrastructure.

## Changes

### 1. Fixed Test Case Failure (Unique Constraint Violation)
- **Issue**: `TestSeeding.test_if_seeding_works` was failing with `UniqueViolation` error when creating participant teams due to duplicate team names generated by Faker
- **Fix**: Modified `create_participant_team()` to accept an optional `team_idx` parameter and include it in team names to ensure uniqueness
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 2. Fixed Non-Interactive Docker Environment Support
- **Issue**: Seeding script was failing in Docker containers with `EOFError: EOF when reading a line` when trying to read user input
- **Fix**: Added `try/except EOFError` handling in `check_database()` to automatically proceed in non-interactive environments
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 3. Fixed All Pylint Errors in seed.py
- **Issue**: Multiple pylint violations including line length, too many arguments, too many locals, unnecessary else statements, etc.
- **Fix**: 
  - Refactored large functions into smaller helper functions (`_create_participant_teams`, `_create_bulk_submissions`, `_create_bulk_leaderboard_data`, `_process_challenge`)
  - Split long lines across multiple lines
  - Removed Python 2 compatibility code (`xrange`)
  - Changed `create_challenge()` to accept a config dictionary instead of many positional arguments
  - Wrapped all file operations with `with` statements
  - Added proper encoding specification for text file operations
  - Fixed inconsistent return statements
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 4. Reduced Test Data Volume
- **Change**: Reduced `NUMBER_OF_SUBMISSIONS` from 10000 to 100 to speed up test execution
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 5. Updated Node.js Version
- **Issue**: npm packages were showing `EBADENGINE` warnings because they require Node 20+ but the Dockerfile was using Node 18
- **Fix**: 
  - Updated Node.js from 18.19.0 to 20.18.0 in both dev and production Dockerfiles
  - Updated `package.json` engines requirement to Node >=20.0.0 and npm >=10.0.0
- **Files**: 
  - `docker/dev/nodejs/Dockerfile`
  - `docker/prod/nodejs/Dockerfile`
  - `package.json`

### 6. Fixed Chrome/Karma Hanging Issue
- **Issue**: Karma tests were hanging after completion, preventing the build from finishing
- **Fix**: 
  - Removed `--remote-debugging-port=9222` flag which was keeping Chrome open
  - Added Chrome flags to prevent background processes from keeping it alive
  - Reduced timeout values to make Chrome exit faster
  - Added cleanup function in entrypoint script to kill Chrome processes on exit
- **Files**: 
  - `karma.conf.js`
  - `docker/dev/nodejs/nodejs-entrypoint.sh`

### 7. Fixed Docker Build Hanging Issue
- **Issue**: `docker-compose run` commands were getting stuck because the entrypoint always started the dev server, even when a command was provided
- **Fix**: Updated entrypoint script to check if arguments are provided and execute them directly instead of always starting the dev server
- **Files**: `docker/dev/nodejs/nodejs-entrypoint.sh`

### 8. Improved Docker Build Resilience
- **Issue**: ARM64 Docker builds were failing due to network timeouts when installing Chromium
- **Fix**: Added retry logic (3 attempts with 10-second delays) for Chromium installation
- **Files**: `docker/dev/nodejs/Dockerfile`

## Testing

### Before
- ❌ Travis CI build failing with unique constraint violation
- ❌ Docker builds hanging and never completing
- ❌ Karma tests hanging after completion
- ❌ Multiple pylint errors

### After
- ✅ All tests passing
- ✅ Docker builds complete successfully
- ✅ Karma tests run and exit cleanly
- ✅ All pylint checks passing
- ✅ Frontend tests can be run with: `docker-compose run nodejs npm test`

## Impact

- **Build Time**: Reduced test execution time by reducing submission count from 10,000 to 100
- **Reliability**: Improved Docker build reliability with retry logic for network issues
- **Code Quality**: All pylint violations resolved, code is more maintainable
- **Compatibility**: Updated to Node.js 20 to meet package requirements

## Related Issues
Fixes Travis CI build failures and Docker build hanging issues.

## Checklist
- [x] All tests pass
- [x] Code follows project style guidelines
- [x] Pylint checks pass
- [x] Docker builds complete successfully
- [x] Frontend tests can be executed
- [x] No breaking changes introduced

items.title: Fix Stripchat model preview images and hover fanart
items.body:
### Motivation
- Model hover previews on Stripchat pages were missing images because the API payloads use varying fields and sometimes protocol-relative or relative image URLs.
- The listing code also didn't always expose the resolved image as `fanart`, so hover/preview panels could show an empty image.

### Description
- Added `_normalize_model_image_url` to canonicalize protocol-relative and relative image URLs and return a safe absolute URL. 
- Added `_model_screenshot` to pick the best available preview field from the model payload or fall back to the snapshot-based thumbnail URL generated from `snapshotTimestamp` and `id`.
- Replaced inline thumbnail construction in `List`, `List2`, and `List3` with calls to `_model_screenshot` and pass the resolved value as both the item thumbnail and the `fanart` argument to `site.add_download_link` for consistent hover previews.
- Updated `tests/sites/test_stripchat.py` to assert that the list items include both `iconimage` and `fanart` and added a test for the snapshot fallback behavior.

### Testing
- Ran `pytest -q tests/sites/test_stripchat.py` and all tests passed (`9 passed`).
- The changes were exercised by unit tests that validate API parsing, thumbnail selection, fanart assignment, and the snapshot fallback logic.

items.title: fix: fragile regex in ReAct agent action parsing
items.body: The previous implementation failed when LLM outputs contained extra newlines or nested braces in JSON strings (common with provider like deepseek). The new parser reliably locates `Action Input:` and extracts JSON using balanced brace counting. Failing case: ``` Thought: I need to calculate the sum of the first 10 numbers. Action: Calculator Tool Action Input: { "code": "print(sum(range(1, 11)))" } ``` I've also moved `test_agent_structured_python_tool.py` to correct directory (requires API keys, was breaking fork PR CI) --- > [!NOTE] > Replaces regex-based `Action Input` parsing with balanced-brace JSON extraction in `Agent._parse_action` and adds unit tests covering varied output formats. > > - **Agent parsing (`dynamiq/nodes/agents/agent.py`)**: > - Replace regex-based `Action Input` parsing with brace-balanced JSON extraction that handles extra newlines, nested braces, and escaped quotes. > - Strip optional markdown code fences from inputs; log warnings on JSON decode errors; raise recoverable error when no valid pairs found. > - Remove dependency on `regex` module. > - **Tests**: > - Add `tests/unit/nodes/agents/test_agent_parsing.py` with cases for DeepSeek-style outputs, standard format, multiple newlines, and JSON wrapped in markdown. > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 204198a02972c84b26283d5ca9979c7f315a665f. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot). ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Improved JSON parsing in `Agent` class to handle nested braces and newlines, with new tests added for verification. > > - **Behavior**: > - Improved `_parse_action()` in `agent.py` to handle JSON extraction using balanced brace counting, fixing issues with extra newlines and nested braces. > - Removed fragile regex pattern for `Action Input` parsing. > - **Tests**: > - Added `test_agent_parsing.py` to test various output formats, including DeepSeek-style, standard format, multiple newlines, and JSON wrapped in markdown. > - Tests ensure correct parsing of `Thought`, `Action`, and `Action Input` fields. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=dynamiq-ai%2Fdynamiq&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for b7e582ddab8fe23632770759c07e66956b7f11cf. items.title: Modernize
items.body:
This commit modernizes the entire Malwarehouse codebase from Python 2.7 to Python 3.9+:

### Core Changes:
- Updated all print statements to print() functions
- Replaced file() built-in with open() using proper binary mode
- Changed exception handling from 'except Exception, e' to 'except Exception as e'
- Updated .iteritems() to .items() for dictionary iteration
- Replaced ConfigParser.SafeConfigParser with configparser.ConfigParser
- Updated metaclass syntax from __metaclass__ to metaclass= parameter

### Library Modernization:
- Replaced urllib2 with requests library for HTTP operations
- Removed poster library dependency (replaced with requests)
- Updated to ppdeep from pydeep for fuzzy hashing
- Pinned modern versions of SQLAlchemy and python-magic

### New Files:
- Added requirements.txt for pip-based dependency management

### Documentation:
- Updated README.md to reflect Python 3.9+ requirement
- Added installation instructions using pip
- Updated library documentation with modern package names

items.title: add pipeline CI
items.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a GitHub Actions CI pipeline and the first unit test suite for the `core.parser` module, alongside a refactoring that extracts shared models and parsing helpers into a dedicated `core/` package. **Key changes:** - New `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with `code-quality` (ruff + bandit) and `business-logic-tests` (pytest + coverage) jobs, triggered on both `push` and `pull_request` to `dev`/`main`. - Four new test files covering `parse_user_story`, `parse_backlog_file`, and internal helpers — all with correct assertions. - `core/models.py` and `core/parser.py` extracted as a shared package consumed by `main.py`, `pipeline/agents.py`, and both integrations. - `pipeline/__init__.py` and `core/__init__.py` added as package entrypoints. **Issues found:** - No `conftest.py` or `pyproject.toml` `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` section configures `pythonpath = ["."]`. CI works because `PYTHONPATH` is set in the workflow, but local `pytest` runs will fail with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'core'` without manually exporting the variable. - `pytest --cov=.` instruments all Python files in the workspace root, including heavyweight pipeline and integration modules that are not exercised by the parser tests — consider scoping coverage to `--cov=core` etc. - `tests/test_parser_helpers.py` imports private underscore-prefixed functions (`_extract`, `_slugify`) directly, coupling tests to internal implementation details.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Mergeable with minor follow-up — test logic is sound but local developer experience and CI coverage scoping need attention. - The new tests are logically correct and the parser refactoring is clean. However, the absence of a `pythonpath` pytest configuration means local development is broken out of the box, the coverage command is noisier than necessary, and several CI issues flagged in earlier review rounds (redundant pip cache, ruff linting notebooks) remain unaddressed. - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` still has a redundant `actions/cache@v4` step in `business-logic-tests` and `ruff check .` without notebook exclusions. `tests/test_parser_basic.py` (and all test files) need a `pythonpath` configuration to work locally.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | .github/workflows/ci.yml | New CI workflow with two jobs: code-quality (ruff + bandit) and business-logic-tests (pytest). Now correctly includes push triggers and excludes notebooks from bandit. However, the business-logic-tests job retains a redundant `actions/cache@v4` step alongside setup-python's built-in cache, `ruff check .` lacks notebook exclusions, and `--cov=.` instruments all source files unnecessarily. | | tests/test_parser_basic.py | New test covering core parser field parsing (ID, type, title, persona, story points, domain). Logic is correct. Missing local pytest pythonpath configuration means tests fail locally without manually setting PYTHONPATH. | | tests/test_parser_helpers.py | New tests for private helpers `_slugify` and `_extract`. Tests are correct but import private underscore-prefixed functions directly, coupling tests to implementation internals. | | main.py | CLI entrypoint refactored into discrete subcommands. The `--model` argument is declared in the pipeline subparser but never forwarded to `run_pipeline`, silently ignoring user input (pre-existing, flagged in previous thread). The `full` command also hardcodes `max_revisions=2` with no user override. | | core/parser.py | Backlog parsing helpers extracted into a dedicated module. Logic is sound: ID preference by work-item-type prefix, Fibonacci story-point validation, section extraction via DOTALL regex, and technical-notes marker filtering all behave as expected by the new tests. | | pipeline/agents.py | Agent node functions for the LangGraph pipeline. Model names are module-level constants (not wired to the CLI's `--model` flag). Classification, synthesis, extraction, generation, validation, and revision flows all look logically correct. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Dev as Developer (push/PR) participant GHA as GitHub Actions participant CQ as code-quality job participant BLT as business-logic-tests job Dev->>GHA: push / pull_request (dev or main) GHA->>CQ: trigger code-quality CQ->>CQ: checkout + setup-python (cached pip) CQ->>CQ: pip install ruff bandit CQ->>CQ: ruff check . (no notebook exclusion ⚠️) CQ->>CQ: ruff format --check . CQ->>CQ: bandit -r . -ll --exclude .git,notebooks,data CQ->>GHA: write step summary GHA->>BLT: trigger (needs: code-quality) BLT->>BLT: checkout + setup-python (cached pip) BLT->>BLT: actions/cache@v4 (redundant ⚠️) BLT->>BLT: pip install -r requirements.txt BLT->>BLT: pip install pytest pytest-cov BLT->>BLT: pytest --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing BLT->>GHA: write test summary ```

Comments Outside Diff (6)

 1. `integrations/github.py`, line 402-412 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/dda42a89f89d9a14ecd647075d888685491ba2ec/integrations/github.py#L402-L412)) **`find_existing_issue` only inspects the first 100 open issues** The endpoint is called with `per_page=100` but without any pagination loop. If the target repository has more than 100 open issues, the de-duplication check will silently miss issues that fall outside the first page, causing `create_issue` to create a duplicate. This is especially problematic because there is no warning logged when the limit is hit. Either paginate through all pages until exhausted, or use GitHub's issue search endpoint (which accepts a `q=` title filter) to perform an exact lookup in a single round-trip: ```python def find_existing_issue(self, title: str) -> int | None: page = 1 while True: response = self.session.get( self._url("issues"), params={"state": "open", "per_page": 100, "page": page}, ) if response.status_code != 200: return None issues = response.json() if not issues: return None for issue in issues: if issue.get("title") == title: return issue["number"] page += 1 ``` 2. `pipeline/agents.py`, line 403-407 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/dda42a89f89d9a14ecd647075d888685491ba2ec/pipeline/agents.py#L403-L407)) **Hard `ValueError` when classification needs human validation** When `classify_input` determines that a human must review the classification (`classification_needs_human_validation = True`), it sets `confirmed_work_item_type` to `None`. The graph in `pipeline/graph.py` has no conditional edge between the classifier and the rest of the pipeline, so `generate_stories` is always invoked. When it is, this guard raises a `ValueError` that bubbles unhandled all the way up through `run_pipeline → app.invoke` and ultimately through `main.py`'s bare `except Exception` handler, which prints an unhelpful message and exits with code 1. Users have no way to know that the input was ambiguous or that the pipeline stopped early waiting for human input. Consider either: 1. Adding a conditional edge in `graph.py` that routes ambiguous classifications to an `END` node with a clear state flag, or 2. Returning a partial/empty result from `generate_stories` with the `classification_needs_human_validation` flag preserved so callers can inspect it. As-is, this edge case is completely invisible to the end user. 3. `pipeline/agents.py`, line 483-485 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/34509226b146b24357073c7fdb4456372eeaaf90/pipeline/agents.py#L483-L485)) **Validator fail-open silently bypasses revision loop** When `json.JSONDecodeError` is raised (i.e., the LLM validator returned malformed output), `story.valid` is forced to `True`. Because `revise_stories` only processes stories where `story.valid is False`, a story that was never actually validated will skip all revision rounds entirely and flow straight through to the final output. A JSON parse error means the validation outcome is *unknown* — not that the story is valid. The safer default is to keep it as invalid (which it already is, since `GeneratedStory.valid` defaults to `False`) so the revisor has a chance to improve it. Consider also adding a diagnostic feedback string so downstream logging captures what happened: 4. `main.py`, line 181-195 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/34509226b146b24357073c7fdb4456372eeaaf90/main.py#L181-L195)) **`full` command hardcodes `max_revisions=2` — not user-controllable** `_command_full` constructs the inner `Namespace` for `_command_pipeline` with a hardcoded `max_revisions=2`. The `full` subparser (lines 94–116) never defines a `--max-revisions` argument, so there is no way for a user invoking `python main.py full ...` to change the number of revision rounds. This is inconsistent with the `pipeline` subcommand, which does expose `--max-revisions`. Consider adding `--max-revisions` to the `full` subparser and threading it through: ```python full_parser.add_argument( "--max-revisions", type=int, default=2, help="Max revision rounds" ) ``` Then in `_command_full`: ```python max_revisions=args.max_revisions, ``` 5. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 123-127 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/f9a4b46dd58050c6f2d6d068539d0b694e86f90b/.github/workflows/ci.yml#L123-L127)) **`--cov=.` includes test files and unrelated modules** `pytest --cov=.` instruments every Python file discovered under the working directory, including the test files themselves and heavyweight modules like `pipeline/agents.py`, `integrations/github.py`, and `integrations/jira.py`. These files import `langchain_mistralai`, `requests`, etc. at module level. While those packages should be installed via `requirements.txt`, the coverage report will be polluted with lines from files that are never exercised by the parser tests. Limiting coverage to just the source packages avoids noise and gives a more meaningful report: 6. `tests/test_parser_basic.py`, line 1 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/f9a4b46dd58050c6f2d6d068539d0b694e86f90b/tests/test_parser_basic.py#L1)) **No `conftest.py` or pytest `pythonpath` configuration for local development** The CI workflow sets `PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}` before running pytest, which is why `from core.parser import ...` resolves correctly in CI. However, there is no `conftest.py`, `pytest.ini`, or `pyproject.toml` section that configures `pythonpath` for local development. Any developer who clones the repo and runs `pytest` without manually exporting `PYTHONPATH=.` will immediately get: ``` ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'core' ``` The simplest fix is to add a minimal `pyproject.toml`: ```toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath = ["."] ``` Or a root-level `conftest.py` with a `sys.path` insert. This would also make the CI step self-documenting rather than relying on an environment variable that is easy to forget. This same issue affects `tests/test_parser_backlog_file.py`, `tests/test_parser_helpers.py`, and `tests/test_parser_user_story.py`.
Last reviewed commit: ["feat: Add bandit sec..."](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/commit/f9a4b46dd58050c6f2d6d068539d0b694e86f90b)

items.title: Modernize DriverBuddy for IDA 7.x+, Ghidra, Binary Ninja, and Radare2
items.body:
The repository contained only a plugin stub for IDA 7.4 with an empty submodule directory. This PR restores the complete toolkit and extends support to all major reverse engineering platforms.

## Platform Support

- **IDA Pro 7.x+**: Updated to Python 3, preserved all original functionality
- **Ghidra 10.x+**: New script with symbol table and decompiler integration
- **Binary Ninja 3.x+**: Plugin using MLIL API for analysis
- **Radare2 5.x+**: Script using r2pipe for command-line workflows

All implementations share core analysis capabilities:
- Driver type detection (WDM/WDF/Mini-Filter/NDIS)
- DispatchDeviceControl location via DRIVER_OBJECT.MajorFunction[0x0E] analysis
- IOCTL extraction and decoding (device type, method, access rights)
- Dangerous function detection (strcpy, memcpy, etc.)
- Windows API tracking (SeAccessCheck, Zw*, ObRef/Deref patterns)

## Python 3 Migration

Restored missing module files from nccgroup/DriverBuddy and converted to Python 3:
- `dict.iteritems()` → `dict.items()`
- `dict.itervalues()` → `dict.values()`
- Print statement syntax
- Fixed chained operator bug in wdm.py structure parser

## Documentation

Complete rewrite with ASCII skull motif (no emojis):
- Installation/usage for each platform
- Real-world analysis examples (IOCTL fuzzing prep, vulnerability discovery)
- Security checklist for driver review

**Example usage (any platform):**
```
[+] Driver type: WDM
[+] Found DispatchDeviceControl at 0x00011234
[+] IOCTL: 0x0022200C
    Device   : FILE_DEVICE_UNKNOWN (0x0022)
    Method   : METHOD_NEITHER  ☠ HIGH RISK
    Access   : FILE_ANY_ACCESS
[+] Found strcpy with 3 references - buffer overflow candidates
```

items.title: correctly handle AWS provider setting config vm_[name] to None
items.body:
### What does this PR do?
Causes salt.config.get_cloud_config_value() to ignore `None` config values at the highest level of precedence: vm_

Setting a vm_[name] config to None or anything else that evaluates to False will still be returned as `value = deepcopy(vm_[name])` 

### What issues does this PR fix or reference?
At some point in the salt-cloud mapfile deployment of AWS EC2 VMs, the aws provider sometimes sets the config value of `vm_[name]` to `None`. Using `None` doesn't seem to make any sense. The documented behavior of `salt.config.get_cloud_config_value()` tries to prefer this over the map, profile, and provider defaults, but fails to `vm_[name].copy()` the value to return the name because it is `None`, which is `NoneType`, and does not implement `.copy()`, and generates an exception to this effect.

    [ERROR   ] There was a profile error: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'copy' Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/cli.py", line 281, in run self.config.get('names')
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/__init__.py", line 1454, in run_profile ret[name] = self.create(vm_)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/__init__.py", line 1284, in create output = self.clouds[func](vm_)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/clouds/ec2.py", line 2760, in create for key, value in six.iteritems(__utils__['cloud.bootstrap'](vm_, __opts__)):

items.title: Implement AI Gateway for intelligent LLM provider routing with cost optimization and auto-fallback
items.body:
Implements multi-provider LLM routing for Jarvis AssistantService with cost optimization (Groq default), context-based escalation (Gemini for >10k tokens/multimodal), and automatic rate-limit fallback.

## Core Implementation

**`AIGateway` (`app/adapters/infrastructure/ai_gateway.py`)**
- Token-based routing: Groq (default) → Gemini (>10k tokens)
- Multimodal detection → Gemini
- Rate limit detection → transparent provider fallback
- Module-level tokenizer caching (tiktoken)

**`GatewayLLMCommandAdapter` (`app/adapters/infrastructure/gateway_llm_adapter.py`)**
- Integrates AI Gateway with existing LLM command interpretation
- Maintains backward compatibility with `LLMCommandAdapter` interface
- Uses Groq for conversational responses, Gemini adapter for function calling

**Container integration (`app/container.py`)**
- Auto-initialization when API keys present
- Graceful degradation to rule-based interpreter
- Debug logging sanitized (removed API key exposure)

## Configuration

```python
# .env
GROQ_API_KEY=your_key
GROQ_MODEL=llama-3.1-70b-versatile
GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key
GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-flash-latest
```

## Usage

```python
# Automatic via Container
container = create_edge_container(use_ai_gateway=True)
assistant = container.assistant_service

# Direct usage
gateway = AIGateway(groq_api_key="...", gemini_api_key="...")
result = gateway.generate_completion(messages)
# Short payloads → Groq (fast, cheap)
# Large payloads (>10k tokens) → Gemini (high capacity)
# Rate limit → auto-fallback
```

## Routing Decision Tree

```
1. Multimodal? → Gemini
2. Tokens > 10k? → Gemini  
3. Groq available? → Groq
4. Fallback → Gemini
5. Rate limit hit → Switch provider
```

## Dependencies

- `groq>=0.4.0` - Groq API client
- `tiktoken>=0.5.0` - Token counting (fallback to char/4 approximation)

## Testing

- 14 new tests covering routing logic, fallback, token counting
- All existing tests passing (74 total)
- CodeQL: 0 vulnerabilities

items.title: Refactor legacy Python Linux automation scripts
items.body:
This commit modernizes the entire HOPE codebase from Python 2 to Python 3, along with several GitHub repository improvements.

Python 2 to 3 Migration:
- Update shebang to python3 in main script
- Convert all print statements to print() function calls
- Replace raw_input() with input()
- Replace optparse with argparse (more modern)
- Decode subprocess output to UTF-8 strings
- Convert zip() results to lists where needed
- Remove deprecated encoding declarations

Repository Improvements:
- Add README.md to root directory with updated documentation
- Update README to reflect Python 3.6+ requirement
- Update README to indicate SLES 12 support
- Add Features section to README
- Update LICENSE copyright year to 2018-2025
- Enhance .gitignore with modern Python patterns
- Add requirements.txt file

All functionality preserved with modernized code. Testing recommended for production use.

# Pull Request Template

Please add:

* A reference to a related issue in your repository;
* A description of the changes proposed in the pull request;
* @mentions of the person or team responsible for reviewing proposed changes.

items.title: Refactor dialog architecture: Remove indirect callbacks and timeouts, add direct typed methods
items.body:
Dialogs were timing out after 30 seconds, interrupting user workflows with "GUI prompt timed out" errors. Additionally, the dialog architecture used an indirect callback pattern with string-based type switching that made the code hard to understand and maintain.

## Changes

**Removed dialog timeout**
- Changed `event.wait(timeout=30.0)` to `event.wait(timeout=None)` in `handle_gui_prompt_threadsafe()`
- Dialogs now wait indefinitely for user interaction

**Added error handling**
- Wrapped `_handle_gui_prompt_on_main_thread()` in try-finally block
- Ensures `event.set()` always called to prevent deadlocks

**Refactored dialog architecture**
- Removed indirect callback pattern with `request_input(gui_type='...', gui_options={...})`
- Added direct typed methods to Utils class:
  - `show_info_dialog(title, message)` - Info dialogs with OK button
  - `show_question_dialog(title, message, default, warning)` - Yes/No questions
  - `show_choice_dialog(title, message, choices, default_value, warning, note)` - Dropdown selections
  - `show_input_dialog(title, message, placeholder)` - Text input
- Changed from `gui_callback` to `gui_handler` pattern
- Auto-fallback to CLI when GUI not available

**Code cleanup**
- Removed unused `QFileDialog` import from `upload_page.py`
- Migrated all dialogs in `kext_maestro.py` (7 dialogs)
- Migrated all dialogs in `hardware_customizer.py` (2 dialogs)

## Architecture Improvement

**Before (messy indirect pattern):**
```python
gui_options = {
    'title': 'Select WiFi Kext',
    'message': 'Choose kext...',
    'choices': [...],
    'default': '1',
    'warning': 'Warning text'
}
result = utils.request_input("Select...", gui_type='choice', gui_options=gui_options)
```

**After (clean direct methods):**
```python
result = utils.show_choice_dialog(
    'Select WiFi Kext',
    'Choose kext...',
    choices=[...],
    default_value='1',
    warning='Warning text'
)
```

## Dialog Inventory

**Custom dialogs** (11 in `custom_dialogs.py`): Input, Choice, Question, Info, SMBIOS, Kexts, ACPI Patches, macOS Version, WiFi Network Count, Codec Layout, Before Using EFI

**File dialogs** (3 using native `QFileDialog`): Hardware report selection, ACPI folder selection, Console log saving

## Benefits

✅ Much cleaner, more maintainable code  
✅ Type-safe parameters with IDE autocomplete  
✅ Easy to understand what dialog will show  
✅ Consistent behavior in GUI and CLI  
✅ No more string-based type dispatching  
✅ No more annoying timeouts

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> go trough the whole codebase and find other Dialog's that are not in the custom_dialogs.py because we want to have a uniform codebase and good looking Dialog's. with amazing styling and good UI/UX experiance for the user.


</details>

items.title: Add main menu display to terminal session
items.body:
Users entering terminal mode (no CLI arguments) saw only a warning message with no visible menu options, leaving them unsure how to proceed.

### Changes

- Added `show_main_menu()` method to `AutoSploitTerminal` class displaying available options and commands
- Called menu display in `terminal_main_display()` after the initial warning

### Result

```
[-] no arguments have been parsed at run time, dropping into terminal session...

Available Options:
==================
1. Usage And Legal      - View usage instructions and legal information
2. Gather Hosts         - Search for hosts using Shodan/ZoomEye/Censys
3. Custom Hosts         - Load a custom hosts file
4. Add Single Host      - Add individual IP addresses
5. View Gathered Hosts  - Display collected target IPs
6. Exploit Gathered Hosts - Launch exploits against gathered hosts
99. Quit               - Exit the program

Commands: search, view, exploit, custom, single, nmap, help, exit

root@autosploit#
```

### Note

Other items from the issue (ZoomEye API key config, `load_api_keys()` ordering, API-KEY header auth) were already correctly implemented in the codebase.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## 问题描述

AutoSploit 存在多个功能缺失和问题需要修复：

### 问题 1：API 密钥配置不完整
- `API_KEYS` 字典缺少 ZoomEye
- ZoomEye 使用硬编码的"借用"凭据，而非用户自己的 API Key
- `load_api_keys()` 函数只处理 Shodan 和 Censys

### 问题 2：终端交互式菜单缺失
输入完 API 后显示：
```
[-] no arguments have been parsed at run time, dropping into terminal session...
```
但没有显示主菜单选项：
```
1. Usage And Legal
2. Gather Hosts
3. Custom Hosts
4. Add Single Host
5. View Gathered Hosts
6. Exploit Gathered Hosts
99. Quit
```

用户不知道如何操作。

### 问题 3：lib/term/terminal.py 兼容性问题
第14-17行的 Python 2/3 兼容代码逻辑错误：
```python
try:
    raw_input
except NameError:
    raw_input = input  # 正确！
```
确保这个逻辑是正确的（目前是对的，但需要确认）

---

## 需要修改的文件

### 1. lib/settings.py

**修改 `API_KEYS` 字典，添加 ZoomEye：**
```python
API_KEYS = {
    "shodan": ("{}/etc/tokens/shodan.key".format(CUR_DIR),),
    "zoomeye": ("{}/etc/tokens/zoomeye.key".format(CUR_DIR),),
    "censys": ("{}/etc/tokens/censys.key".format(CUR_DIR), "{}/etc/tokens/censys.id".format(CUR_DIR)),
}
```

**修改 `load_api_keys()` 函数：**
- 按固定顺序提示输入（shodan → zoomeye → censys）
- 为每个 API 显示清晰的输入提示
- 返回包含三个 API 的完整 token 字典：
```python
api_tokens = {
    "shodan": (shodan_key,),
    "zoomeye": (zoomeye_key,),
    "censys": (censys_key, censys_id)
}
```

### 2. api_calls/zoomeye.py

**修改 ZoomEyeAPIHook 类，使用用户的 API Key：**

当前代码使用硬编码凭据登录：
```python
def __get_auth(self):
    username = self.__decode(self.user_file)
    password = self.__decode(self.pass_file)
    ...
```

需要修改为：
1. 在 `__init__` 方法中接收 `token` 参数（保持与其他 API 一致的接口）
2. 删除 `__decode` 和 `__get_auth` 方法
3. 修改 `search` 方法使用 API-KEY 头部认证：
```python
headers = {"API-KEY": self.token}
if self.user_agent:
    headers["User-Agent"] = self.user_agent["User-Agent"]
```

### 3. lib/term/terminal.py

**在 `terminal_main_display` 方法中添加主菜单显示：**

在进入主循环之前，显示可用选项的菜单：
```python
def show_main_menu(self):
    """显示主菜单选项"""
    menu = """
Available Options:
==================
1. Usage And Legal      - View usage instructions and legal information
2. Gather Hosts         - Search for hosts using Shodan/ZoomEye/Censys
3. Custom Hosts         - Load a custom hosts file
4. Add Single Host      - Add individual IP addresses
5. View Gathered Hosts  - Display collected target IPs
6. Exploit Gathered Hosts - Launch exploits against gathered hosts
99. Quit               - Exit the program

Commands: search, view, exploit, custom, single, nmap, help, exit
"""
    print(menu)
```

在 `terminal_main_display` 方法开头调用此方法显示菜单。

**修改 `do_api_search` 方法中的 ZoomEye 调用：**
确保正确传递 API token：
```python
elif api.lower() == "zoomeye":
    token = tokens["zoomeye"][0]
```

### 4. lib/term/terminal.py 中的 `do_token_reset` 方法

确保能正确重置 ZoomEye 的 token：
```python
elif api.lower() == "zoomeye":
    lib.output.info("resetting zoomeye API credentials")
    with open(lib.settings.API_KEYS["zoomeye"][0], 'w') as token_:
        token_.write(token)
```

---

## 各 API 认证方式说明

| API | 需要的凭据 | 认证方式 |
|-----|-----------|---------|
| **Shodan** | API Key | URL 参数 `?key=xxx` |
| **ZoomEye** | API Key | HTTP Header `API-KEY: xxx` |
| **Censys** | API ID + Secret | HTTP Basic Auth |

---

## 预期效果

### 启动时提示输入 API 密钥：
```
[?] enter your Shodan API token: xxx
[?] enter your Zoomeye API token: xxx
[?] enter your Censys API token: xxx
[?] enter your Censys ID: xxx
```

### 显示主菜单：
```
[-] no arguments have been parsed at run time, dropping into terminal session...

Available Options:
==================
1. Usage And Legal      - View usage instructions and legal information
2. Gather Hosts         - Search for hosts using Shodan/ZoomEye/Censys
3. Custom Hosts         - Load a custom hosts file
4. Add Single Host      - Add individual IP addresses
5. View Gathered Hosts  - Display collected target IPs
6. Exploit Gathered Hosts - Launch exploits against gathered hosts
99. Quit               - Exit the program

Commands: search, view, exploit, custom, single, nmap, help, exit

root@autosploit#
```

### 用户可以使用命令：
- `search shodan,zoomeye,censys apache` - 使用三个引擎搜索
- `view` - 查看收集的主机
- `exploit 127.0.0.1 4444 default` - 对目标发起攻击
- `help` - 显示帮助信息

</details>

items.body:
Automated ship via scripts/ship_main.py

- Runs local strict gate stack
- Watches required checks until all pass
- Merges with branch cleanup

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Ships a unified quality gate contract and keeps the symbol gate advisory on main, while expanding orchestration (authority escalation, disposition enforcement, delivery bus, compliance, cycles, repertoire) and NSIL. Adds toolchain manifest checks, aligns CI/ship flows to the contract, and brings the test suite to green.

- **New Features**
  - Orchestration: authority escalation engine; disposition enforcer; delivery bus; compliance tracking; cycle detection; repertoire with proven-resolution hints; reporter adds cycle alerts/disposition; session transfer and knowledge store.
  - NSIL: deterministic local action verification, streaming guard, policy grammar compiler, vLLM/Ollama adapters, training data pipeline; new nsil_* MCP tools.
  - Gate contract as source of truth: explicit advisory policy (PRs always advisory; main and ship branches advisory), ship base strategy=merge-base; CI triggers on PR and main only; tests/workflows/pre‑push read gate_contract.yaml.
  - Tooling: toolchain manifest module + toolchain_health_check MCP tool; pre‑push warns on missing tools with one‑command install; structure channel split (discovery/orphans) with orphan/re‑export improvements; docs updated for 68 tools.

- **Refactors**
  - Reduced cognitive complexity across mutation engine/ci_stats/nsil training/discovery; quality_helpers split into mcp_tools/quality with a backward‑compatible facade.
  - API hardening and test remediation: updated finalize/extract_finding_indexes signatures, compass save validation, behavior compass guards, transfer packet handling; all tests fixed (5185 passed, 0 failed).

<sup>Written for commit 84b5f53df762ad29a769c67e94b8afbb769e083b. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

items.title: feat(swe-bench): BDI 三模式评估 (baseline/bdi/bdi-repair)
items.body:
## Summary

将 PlanBench/TravelPlanner 的 BDI 三层验证 + repair 闭环完整移植到 SWE-bench 域。

### 新增文件 (`src/bdi_llm/swe_bench/`)
- `adapter.py` — SWEBenchTaskAdapter (dataset → PlanningTask)
- `signatures.py` — GeneratePlanCodingBaseline + RepairPlanCoding
- `engine.py` — SWEBenchGenerator (baseline/bdi/repair 三方法)
- `feedback.py` — pytest 输出解析 + 多层验证上下文构建
- `runner.py` — `evaluate_sample()` 完整闭环含 repair loop × 3

### 修改文件
- `coding_planner.py` — 添加 `generate_plan_baseline()`
- `run_swe_bench_batch.py` — 添加 `--execution_mode` + `--max_repair_attempts`

### 测试
- 18/18 新测试通过 (adapter 9 + feedback 9)
- 5/5 原有 harness 测试通过，零回归

### 三模式工作流
```
[baseline]    Predict → plan → execute → test → 报告
[bdi]         CoT → plan → structural verify → execute → test → 报告
[bdi-repair]  CoT → plan → SV → execute → test → {失败→反馈→repair}×3
```

### CLI 用法
```bash
python scripts/swe_bench/run_swe_bench_batch.py --execution_mode bdi-repair --max_repair_attempts 3
```

## Summary by Sourcery

为 SWE-bench 集成完整的基于 BDI 的规划、验证与修复工作流，新增统一的 SWE-bench 运行器，并通过批处理 CLI 暴露多模式执行（baseline/BDI/BDI-repair），同时扩展编码与评估工具。

New Features:
- 添加 SWE-bench 任务适配器，用于将基准实例规范化为共享 BDI 底层使用的 `PlanningTask` 对象。
- 引入 SWE-bench 生成引擎和运行器，支持 baseline、BDI 和 BDI-repair 模式，并具备迭代式的测试驱动修复能力。
- 提供结构化反馈工具，用于提取 `pytest` 失败信息，并为修复提示构建多层验证上下文。
- 在 SWE-bench 批处理脚本中暴露执行模式和修复尝试次数控制，并增加一次性成功与修复后成功的汇总指标。
- 添加用于论文撰写的框架文档，包括方法、PDDL 与 TravelPlanner 结果、溯源策略以及摘要/贡献规划等内容。

Enhancements:
- 扩展编码 BDI 规划器，加入 baseline（非 CoT）规划生成路径，以及显式的编码域规范。
- 优化 SWE-bench 批处理框架集成，使其使用新的评估运行器以及项目根目录环境加载。
- 为 SWE-bench 适配器和反馈模块添加单元测试，以验证解析和验证上下文构建的正确性。

Documentation:

## Summary by Sourcery

Integrate a full BDI-based planning, verification, and repair workflow for SWE-bench, add a unified SWE-bench runner, and expose multi-mode execution (baseline/BDI/BDI-repair) through the batch CLI while extending coding and evaluation utilities.

New Features:
- Add SWE-bench task adapter to normalize benchmark instances into PlanningTask objects for the shared BDI substrate.
- Introduce a SWE-bench generation engine and runner supporting baseline, BDI, and BDI-repair modes with iterative test-driven repair.

<issue_title>[EPIC] - `repo_lint` Improvement Plan</issue_title>
<issue_description># [EPIC] - `repo_lint` Improvement Plan

This plan outlines prioritized phases to address all findings. Each item includes context, affected components, and suggested fixes.

---

## Phase 1 – Critical Fixes and Corrections (High Priority)

- [ ] **Fix repository root detection** (Severity: **High**)  
  - **Context:** `get_repo_root()` and `find_repo_root()` currently require a `.git` directory, causing failures outside Git worktrees.  
  - **Affected Files:** `tools/repo_lint/install/install_helpers.py`, `tools/repo_lint/runners/base.py` (or wherever `find_repo_root` is defined).  
  - **Fix Steps:** Modify these functions to check for `.git` but **if not found**, return the current working directory as root (or use an environment override). For example:

    ~~~python
    root = current
    while current != current.parent:
        if (current / ".git").exists():
            root = current
            break
        current = current.parent
    return root
    ~~~

    Ensure `find_repo_root` in all runners uses this updated logic.  
  - **Rationale:** Allows `repo_lint` to run in directories without Git, matching user expectations.

- [ ] **Clarify exit codes for unsafe mode** (Severity: **High**)  
  - **Context:** `repo_lint fix --unsafe` in CI or without `--yes-i-know` uses exit code 2 (“Missing tools”), which is misleading.  
  - **Affected Files:** `tools/repo_lint/cli.py`, in `cmd_fix`.  
  - **Fix Steps:** Introduce a new exit code (e.g. `ExitCode.UNSAFE_VIOLATION = 4`) or reuse code 3 for policy errors. Change the `return ExitCode.MISSING_TOOLS` on lines 22 and 36 of `cmd_fix` to this new code. Update `ExitCode` enum accordingly. Adjust help text to note this code.  
  - **Rationale:** Distinguishes configuration errors from missing tools, making CI logs clearer.

- [ ] **Handle partial install failures gracefully** (Severity: **Medium**)  
  - **Context:** In `cmd_install`, if Python tool installation fails, the script still prints next-step instructions but ends with an error. This could confuse users.  
  - **Affected Files:** `tools/repo_lint/cli.py`, lines around 45–60 in `cmd_install`.  
  - **Fix Steps:** Change logic so that the overall success flag (`success`) is computed across all sections: if Python tools or any manual section fails, show errors but consider whether to continue. For example, do **not** `return ExitCode.INTERNAL_ERROR` immediately upon Python failure; instead, gather errors and only exit at end. Alternatively, exit early but skip printing irrelevant instructions. Update output messages accordingly.  
  - **Rationale:** Improves user experience during tool setup and makes failure reasons clear.

- [ ] **Ensure missing docstring validator is detected** (Severity: **Medium**)  
  - **Context:** If `validate_docstrings.py` is missing, runners return a violation with an “script not found” error. This may be unclear.  
  - **Affected Files:** `tools/repo_lint/runners/*_runner.py` (each `_run_docstring_validation` implementation).  
  - **Fix Steps:** In `_run_docstring_validation` methods, catch the “script not found” case explicitly. Print a clear error (`"Docstring validation skipped: script not found"`). Optionally, raise a `MissingToolError`. Document this case in README.  
  - **Rationale:** Makes it obvious when docstring checks are not executed.

- [ ] **Validate non-Python unsafe mode behavior** (Severity: **Medium**)  
  - **Context:** The `--unsafe` fix path only processes Python files. If a user passes `--only=perl` with `--unsafe`, the logic will not handle it.  
  - **Affected Files:** `tools/repo_lint/cli.py`, `cmd_fix` unsafe section.  
  - **Fix Steps:** Restrict `--unsafe` to only allow `--only=python` (error out otherwise), or extend unsafe fixer support to other languages (if implemented). At minimum, add a check:

    ~~~python
    if unsafe_mode and args.only and args.only != "python":
        print("❌ Unsafe fixes not supported for this language", file=sys.stderr)
        return ExitCode.MISSING_TOOLS
    ~~~

  - **Rationale:** Prevents silent no-op when unsupported combinations are requested.

- [ ] **Add missing unit tests for error conditions** (Severity: **Low**)  
  - **Context:** Key error branches (missing tools, missing policy file, unsafe mode cases) lack automated tests.  
  - **Affected Files:** Add to `tools/repo_lint/tests/`.  
  - **Fix Steps:** Write tests using `pytest` to simulate: invoking `repo_lint check` without tools (expect exit code 2), `repo_lint fix` with `--unsafe` in CI (exit 2), missing policy (exit 3), etc. Use subprocess calls or invoke `main()` directly.  
  - **Rationale:** Catches regressions early and ensures CLI behavior is documented.

items.title: Migrate Cython extensions from Python 2 to Python 3
items.body:
The three Cython files in `src/cy_ext` (CSeqDict, gem_qa, Delta) were written for Python 2 and fail to compile under Python 3/Cython 3.x.

## Python 2 → 3 syntax fixes

- **Print statements**: `print 'msg'` → `print('msg')`
- **Dict iteration**: `.iteritems()` → `.items()`, `.iterkeys()` → `.keys()`
- **String encoding**: Added UTF-8 encoding for `fopen()` since Python 3 strings are unicode by default

## Cython 3.x compatibility

- **CSeqDict.pxd**: Fixed `cpdef cppclass` → `cdef cppclass` (invalid syntax in modern Cython)
- **CSeqDict.pyx**: Added inline `cdef` attribute declarations and `cdef extern` struct definition for `SeqIDLL` to satisfy stricter type checking

## Build system modernization

- **setup.py**: Updated to use modern Python 3 build tools
  - Replaced deprecated `distutils.core` with `setuptools`
  - Replaced deprecated `Cython.Distutils.build_ext` with `Cython.Build.cythonize`
  - Removed invalid `include_path` parameter
  - Added `language_level: "3"` compiler directive
  - Improved code formatting

## Build artifacts

Updated `.gitignore` to exclude Cython-generated C/C++ files.

---

All modules now compile and import successfully under Python 3.12.

items.title: fix: Prevent SystemExit race condition during graceful shutdown (Issue #1314)
items.body:
## Summary

Fixes race condition where pressing Ctrl+C causes `SystemExit` exception in atexit callback, producing an ugly stack trace.

## Problem

When users press Ctrl+C:
1. Signal handler calls `sys.exit(0)`
2. atexit triggers `_cleanup_on_exit` callback
3. Stop hook tries to read from stdin via `sys.stdin.read()`
4. The pending `sys.exit(0)` interrupts stdin read → **SystemExit exception**

**Before Fix:**
```
^C
Received interrupt signal. Shutting down...
Exception ignored in atexit callback: <bound method ClaudeLauncher._cleanup_on_exit...>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
  File ".../hook_processor.py", line 145, in read_input
    raw_input = sys.stdin.read()
SystemExit: 0
```

## Solution

Add shutdown flag to skip stdin reads during interrupt:

1. **Signal Handler**: Set `AMPLIHACK_SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS=1` before `sys.exit(0)`
2. **Hook Processor**: Check flag in `read_input()`, return `{}` if shutdown in progress
3. **Result**: No stdin read attempted, no SystemExit exception

**After Fix:**
```
^C
Received interrupt signal. Shutting down...
(Clean exit, no exception)
```

## Changes

**Files Modified:** 3
- `src/amplihack/launcher/core.py` - Set shutdown flag in signal handler
- `.claude/tools/amplihack/hooks/hook_processor.py` - Check flag, skip stdin
- `tests/unit/test_hook_processor_run.py` - Add 2 unit tests

**Lines Changed:** +71 insertions

## Testing

- ✅ Pre-commit hooks passed
- ✅ Unit tests added for shutdown flag behavior
- 🔄 CI will run full test suite
- 📋 Manual verification needed: Press Ctrl+C and verify no stack trace

## Philosophy Compliance

- **Ruthless Simplicity**: 2-line changes per file, minimal complexity
- **Zero-BS**: No stubs, works immediately
- **Modular**: Clean separation via environment variable
- **Clear**: Comments explain the "why"

## Impact

- **Severity**: Medium (cosmetic but poor UX)
- **Users Affected**: All users who press Ctrl+C
- **Risk**: Low (additive change, only affects interrupt path)
- **Cleanup**: All cleanup functionality preserved

Closes #1314

items.title: Implement SessionContext and sequential multi-agent workflow (FR#005)
items.body:
Implements the core multi-agent workflow system using ADK patterns and A2A Protocol with sequential execution and Firestore persistence.

## Changes

### Core Models
- **`backend/models/context_model.py`** - Pydantic `SessionContext` model with required fields: `raw_input`, `summary_text`, `key_entities`, `relationships`, `graph_json`
- **`backend/models/context_model.py`** - `EntityRelationship` model for typed relationship data
- Added `STANDARD_AGENT_ORDER` constant to centralize execution sequence

### Persistence Layer
- **`backend/services/context_persistence.py`** - Firestore service for SessionContext CRUD operations in `agent_context` collection
- Graceful handling when Firestore unavailable
- Session ID validation

### Workflow Engine
- **`backend/agents/base_agent.py`** - New `execute_sequential_workflow()` method enforces strict execution order: Orchestrator → Summarizer → Linker → Visualizer
- Automatic result mapping from agent outputs to SessionContext fields
- Context persisted to Firestore after each agent step
- Type validation for SessionContext parameter

### API Integration
- **`backend/main.py`** - Updated `/api/analyze` endpoint to use SessionContext
- Response includes workflow metadata (session_id, status, errors, persistence status)

### Testing & Documentation
- **`backend/test_fr005_workflow.py`** - Test suite covering SessionContext, workflow execution, and persistence
- **`backend/demo_fr005.py`** - Interactive demo script
- **`docs/FR005_*.md`** - Implementation guide, summary, and quick reference

## Example Usage

```python
from models.context_model import SessionContext
from agents import AgentWorkflow, OrchestratorAgent, SummarizerAgent, LinkerAgent, VisualizerAgent

# Create context
context = SessionContext(
    session_id="session_001",
    raw_input="Document content...",
    content_type="document"
)

# Execute sequential workflow
workflow = AgentWorkflow()
# ... register agents ...
result = await workflow.execute_sequential_workflow(context)

# Access results
print(result.summary_text)        # From Summarizer
print(result.key_entities)        # From Linker
print(result.relationships)       # From Linker
print(result.graph_json)          # From Visualizer
```

## Implementation Notes

- Sequential execution replaces parallel workflow for predictable state updates
- Each agent step includes Firestore persistence for fault tolerance
- Workflow continues on individual agent failures with error tracking
- `STANDARD_AGENT_ORDER` constant eliminates hardcoded agent lists
- Type validation prevents runtime errors from invalid context types

**Lines:** +833 Python code, +28KB documentation  
**Security:** No CodeQL alerts  
**Tests:** All passing

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


----

*This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

<issue_title>Feature Request #005: Core Multi-Agent Workflow Implementation (ADK + A2A Protocol)</issue_title>
<issue_description>Summary Feature Request stevei101/agentnav#5 needs to be the technical implementation of the core multi-agent logic that fulfills the entire vision of the **Agentic Navigator**. Based on the project story, the architecture requires the **Orchestrator Agent** to manage a sequential workflow using the **A2A Protocol** to pass a shared context object between the **Summarizer**, **Linker**, and **Visualizer** agents.

Here is Feature Request stevei101/agentnav#5, defining the initial workflow implementation using the ADK and A2A Protocol.

***

# Feature Request stevei101/agentnav#5: Core Multi-Agent Workflow Implementation (ADK + A2A Protocol)

**Feature Status:** Ideation & Planning
**Priority:** Highest
**Timeline:** 2 Weeks
**Assigned To:** [TBD]

---

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

The entire "Agentic Navigator" concept—our elevator pitch and project story—rests on the successful, collaborative execution of a multi-agent team. Currently, we have the infrastructure (Cloud Run, Podman, Firestore) and the service definitions (ADK structure), but the core business logic of how the agents talk to each other and process data is not yet implemented. The agents are defined, but they do not yet **collaborate** using the **Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol**.

**The Problem:**
- The Orchestrator Agent currently only receives the user input and does not delegate the task sequentially.
- There is no formal `SessionContext` object being passed between agents via A2A.
- The Summarizer, Linker, and Visualizer agents are not yet performing their specialized, collaborative tasks.
- We cannot demonstrate the core value proposition: *a team of agents working together to transform complex input into a visual knowledge graph.*

**I'm always frustrated when** I have to:
- Manually simulate the flow of data between agents.
- Debug the entire process in one monolithic function instead of specialized ADK agents.
- Explain the A2A Protocol concept without a working demonstration.

---

## Describe the solution you'd like

Implement the sequential, collaborative core workflow within the FastAPI Backend using the **Google Agent Development Kit (ADK)** and the **Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol** with a persistent **Firestore Context**.

### 1. **Define the Shared Session Context (Pydantic)**
- Create a Pydantic model (`backend/models/context_model.py`) for the shared `SessionContext`.
- This model must hold all intermediate data, including:
  - `raw_input: str` (Original document/codebase)
  - `summary_text: str` (Output from Summarizer Agent)
  - `key_entities: list[str]` (Output from Linker Agent)
  - `relationships: list[dict]` (Output from Linker Agent: e.g., `{'source': 'A', 'target': 'B', 'type': 'relates_to'}`)
  - `graph_json: dict` (Final visualization-ready output from Visualizer Agent)

### 2. **Implement the Orchestrator Agent (The Brain)**
- Update the `OrchestratorAgent` (`backend/agents/orchestrator.py`) to manage the workflow:
  1. Initialize the `SessionContext` with `raw_input`.
  2. Use the **A2A Protocol** to send the context to the **Summarizer Agent**.
  3. Wait for the A2A response/completion.
  4. Pass the updated context to the **Linker Agent**.
  5. Wait for the A2A response/completion.
  6. Pass the updated context to the **Visualizer Agent**.
  7. Return the final `SessionContext` content (summary + graph JSON) to the user.

### 3. **Implement Specialized Agent Logic (ADK + A2A)**
Update the three specialized agents to perform their specific role, update the context, and pass it on:

| Agent | Task | Input | Output to Context |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Summarizer** | Generate a concise, human-readable summary. | `SessionContext.raw_input` | `SessionContext.summary_text` |
| **Linker** | Identify key entities and the relationships between them (the "graph data"). | `SessionContext.raw_input` + `SessionContext.summary_text` | `SessionContext.key_entities`, `SessionContext.relationships` |
| **Visualizer** | Transform the structured relationship data into the final JSON format for the React dashboard to render. | `SessionContext.relationships` | `SessionContext.graph_json` |

### 4. **Firestore Context Persistence**
- Integrate the `agent_context/` Firestore collection to persist the `SessionContext` object at each step of the workflow. This ensures context is maintained even if an agent's process fails or is interrupted.

### 5. **Gemini / Gemma Integration**
- Integrate the Gemini or Gemma model (via a high-level service call) into the **Linker Agent** to perform the reasoning necessary to extract entities and relationships from the text. This will be the first use of the core AI models.

---

## Describe alternatives you've considered

### Alternative 1: **Monolithic Agent*...

</details>

- Fixes stevei101/agentnav#6

-   [#​3206](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3206): Changed `setuptools.convert_path` to an internal function that is not exposed
    as part of setuptools API.
    Future releases of `setuptools` are likely to remove this function.

Changes
^^^^^^^

-   [#​3202](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3202): Changed behaviour of auto-discovery to not explicitly expand `package_dir`
    for flat-layouts and to not use relative paths starting with `./`.
-   [#​3203](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3203): Prevented `pyproject.toml` parsing from overwriting
    `dist.include_package_data` explicitly set in `setup.py` with default
    value.
-   [#​3208](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3208): Added a warning for non existing files listed with the `file` directive in
    `setup.cfg` and `pyproject.toml`.
-   [#​3208](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3208): Added a default value for dynamic `classifiers` in `pyproject.toml` when
    files are missing and errors being ignored.
-   [#​3211](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3211): Disabled auto-discovery when distribution class has a `configuration`
    attribute (e.g. when the `setup.py` script contains `setup(...,
    configuration=...)`).  This is done to ensure extension-only packages created
    with `numpy.distutils.misc_util.Configuration` are not broken by the safe
    guard
    behaviour to avoid accidental multiple top-level packages in a flat-layout.

    .. note::
    Users that don't set `packages`, `py_modules`, or `configuration` are
    still likely to observe the auto-discovery behavior, which may halt the
    build if the project contains multiple directories and/or multiple Python
    files directly under the project root.

    To disable auto-discovery please explicitly set either `packages` or
    `py_modules`. Alternatively you can also configure :ref:`custom-discovery`.

### [`v61.0.0`](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/HEAD/CHANGES.rst#v6100)

[Compare Source](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/compare/v60.10.0...v61.0.0)

Deprecations
^^^^^^^^^^^^

-   [#​3068](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3068): Deprecated `setuptools.config.read_configuration`,
    `setuptools.config.parse_configuration` and other functions or classes
    from `setuptools.config`.

    Users that still need to parse and process configuration from `setup.cfg` can
    import a direct replacement from `setuptools.config.setupcfg`, however this
    module is transitional and might be removed in the future
    (the `setup.cfg` configuration format itself is likely to be deprecated in the future).

Breaking Changes
^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

-   [#​2894](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/2894): If you purposefully want to create an *"empty distribution"*, please be aware
    that some Python files (or general folders) might be automatically detected and
    included.

    Projects that currently don't specify both `packages` and `py_modules` in their
    configuration and contain extra folders or Python files (not meant for distribution),
    might see these files being included in the wheel archive or even experience
    the build to fail.

    You can check details about the automatic discovery (and how to configure a
    different behaviour) in :doc:`/userguide/package_discovery`.
-   [#​3067](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3067): If the file `pyproject.toml` exists and it includes project
    metadata/config (via `[project]` table or `[tool.setuptools]`),
    a series of new behaviors that are not backward compatible may take place:

    -   The default value of `include_package_data` will be considered to be `True`.
    -   Setuptools will attempt to validate the `pyproject.toml` file according
        to PEP 621 specification.
    -   The values specified in `pyproject.toml` will take precedence over those
        specified in `setup.cfg` or `setup.py`.

Changes
^^^^^^^

-   [#​2887](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/2887): **\[EXPERIMENTAL]** Added automatic discovery for `py_modules` and `packages`
    \-- by :user:`abravalheri`.

    Setuptools will try to find these values assuming that the package uses either
    the *src-layout* (a `src` directory containing all the packages or modules),
    the *flat-layout* (package directories directly under the project root),
    or the *single-module* approach (an isolated Python file, directly under
    the project root).

    The automatic discovery will also respect layouts that are explicitly
    configured using the `package_dir` option.

    For backward-compatibility, this behavior will be observed **only if both**
    `py_modules` **and** `packages` **are not set**.
    (**Note**: specifying `ext_modules` might also prevent auto-discover from
    taking place)

    If setuptools detects modules or packages that are not supposed to be in the
    distribution, please manually set `py_modules` and `packages` in your
    `setup.cfg` or `setup.py` file.
    If you are using a *flat-layout*, you can also consider switching to
    *src-layout*.
-   [#​2887](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/2887): **\[EXPERIMENTAL]** Added automatic configuration for the `name` metadata
    \-- by :user:`abravalheri`.

    Setuptools will adopt the name of the top-level package (or module in the case
    of single-module distributions), **only when** `name` **is not explicitly
    provided**.

    Please note that it is not possible to automatically derive a single name when
    the distribution consists of multiple top-level packages or modules.
-   [#​3066](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3066): Added vendored dependencies for :pypi:`tomli`, :pypi:`validate-pyproject`.

    These dependencies are used to read `pyproject.toml` files and validate them.
-   [#​3067](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3067): **\[EXPERIMENTAL]** When using `pyproject.toml` metadata,
    the default value of `include_package_data` is changed to `True`.
-   [#​3068](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3068): **\[EXPERIMENTAL]** Add support for `pyproject.toml` configuration
    (as introduced by :pep:`621`). Configuration parameters not covered by
    standards are handled in the `[tool.setuptools]` sub-table.

    In the future, existing `setup.cfg` configuration
    may be automatically converted into the `pyproject.toml` equivalent before taking effect
    (as proposed in [#​1688](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/1688)). Meanwhile users can use automated tools like
    :pypi:`ini2toml` to help in the transition.

    Please note that the legacy backend is not guaranteed to work with
    `pyproject.toml` configuration.

    \-- by :user:`abravalheri`
-   [#​3125](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3125): Implicit namespaces (as introduced in :pep:`420`) are now considered by default
    during :doc:`package discovery </userguide/package_discovery>`, when
    `setuptools` configuration and project metadata are added to the
    `pyproject.toml` file.

    To disable this behaviour, use `namespaces = False` when explicitly setting
    the `[tool.setuptools.packages.find]` section in `pyproject.toml`.

    This change is backwards compatible and does not affect the behaviour of
    configuration done in `setup.cfg` or `setup.py`.
-   [#​3152](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3152): **\[EXPERIMENTAL]** Added support for `attr:` and `cmdclass` configurations
    in `setup.cfg` and `pyproject.toml` when `package_dir` is implicitly
    found via auto-discovery.
-   [#​3178](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3178): Postponed importing `ctypes` when hiding files on Windows.
    This helps to prevent errors in systems that might not have `libffi` installed.
-   [#​3179](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3179): Merge with [pypa/distutils@`267dbd2`](https://togithub.com/pypa/distutils/commit/267dbd25ac)

Documentation changes
^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

-   [#​3172](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3172): Added initial documentation about configuring `setuptools` via `pyproject.toml`
    (using standard project metadata).

Misc
^^^^

-   [#​3065](https://togithub.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/3065): Refactored `setuptools.config` by separating configuration parsing (specific
    to the configuration file format, e.g. `setup.cfg`) and post-processing
    (which includes directives such as `file:` that can be used across different
    configuration formats).

items.title: fix GROMACS easyblock for list-type configopts
items.body:
GROMACS easyblock currently fails in `sanity_check_step` when used
with a list-type `configopts` config like:

```
configopts = [
    "-DGMX_THREAD_MPI=OFF -DGMX_DOUBLE=OFF",
    "-DGMX_THREAD_MPI=OFF -DGMX_DOUBLE=ON",
]
```

This patch fixes this issue by correctly iterating over multiple
configopts strings to determine which binary and lib names to
check for.
